title: feat(vault): Lot 5 + Lot 8 — watch daemon + multi-format exports
summary: Implements Lot 5 (vault watch daemon for incremental polling) and Lot 8 (6-format graph exports: HTML/D3.js, Obsidian, Wiki, SVG, GraphML, Neo4j Cypher).

files_changed.purpose: Core watcher module with watchOnce() for single cycles, watchStatus() for daemon status, and watchLoop() for background polling
key_functions: watchOnce(vaultPath, updateFn, graphFn) — Synchronous cycle with SHA256 manifest hashing
watchStatus(vaultPath) — Check daemon status via PID
watchLoop(vaultPath, intervalSec, updateFn, graphFn) — Background polling with signal handling

files_changed.purpose: Daemon launcher script spawned detached from bin/vault.js
files_changed.key_logic: Parses --vault-path and --interval args, calls watchLoop() with polling interval clamped to min 5s

files_changed.purpose: Added watch command dispatch and helper functions
changes: Added imports: spawn from child_process, watchOnce/watchStatus from src/vault/watch.js
Added startVaultWatch(cwd, intervalSec) — spawns daemon detached with stdio:ignore
Added stopVaultWatch(cwd) — kills PID via SIGTERM, removes watch.json
Added onceVaultWatch(cwd) — synchronous cycle with update/graph callbacks
Added printWatch* functions for formatted output
Added 'watch' dispatch in runVault with actions: once|start|stop|status

files_changed.purpose: Added 6 tests for Lot 5 + 7 tests for Lot 8 functionality
tests_lot5: vault watch status sans daemon retourne { active: false }
vault watch start creates watch.json with pid
tests_lot8: vault export --html sans graph.json retourne erreur
vault export --html génère un fichier avec "Peter Knowledge Graph"
vault export --graphml génère un fichier avec balise <graphml
vault export --obsidian génère des fichiers .md dans vault/index/obsidian/
vault export --neo4j génère nodes.cypher et edges.cypher
vault export --svg génère un fichier SVG
vault export --wiki génère vault/index/wiki/index.md et répertoires par type

files_changed.purpose: Added 6 export format handlers (+541 lines for Lot 8)
export_functions: exportHtmlGraph(cwd, graph) — D3.js force-directed interactive graph (1200x900px SVG, hover, click highlighting, legend)
exportObsidianVault(cwd, graph) — ~500 .md files with bidirectional links [[id]] syntax, valid types filter
exportWikiVault(cwd, graph) — Hierarchical Markdown structure with index.md + per-type subdirectories
exportSvgGraph(cwd, graph) — Circular static layout (central nodes inner 150px, others 400px radius)
exportGraphML(cwd, graph) — XML standard format with proper entity escaping (", <, >)
exportNeo4jCypher(cwd, graph) — Cypher CREATE statements with sharding (max 10k per file) + import.sh
exportVault(cwd, format) — Orchestrator checking graph.json exists, dispatching to handler
printExport(result, format, cwd) — Colored CLI feedback per format
files_changed.dispatch_logic: Added 'export' subcommand dispatch checking argv for --html/--obsidian/--wiki/--svg/--graphml/--neo4j flags

architecture.polling_strategy: File hashing via SHA256 of all vault/*.md files, stored in .peter/state.json lastWatchManifest
architecture.daemon_lifecycle: spawn detached + stdio:ignore, PID file in .peter/watch.json, SIGTERM cleanup
architecture.intervals: Configurable via --interval, clamped to min 5s, max ~30s default
architecture.event_logging: Appends to .peter/events.jsonl with watch_cycle events including elapsed, changedFiles, updateOk, graphOk

test_coverage.gate_status: PASSED — secrets, lint, build, tests all clean

review_focus: Lot 5 — Daemon spawning logic: verify detached and stdio handling is correct
Lot 5 — PID checking: process.kill(pid, 0) for validation
Lot 5 — Signal handlers: SIGTERM/SIGINT cleanup in watchLoop
Lot 5 — File hashing: SHA256 manifest comparison for change detection
Lot 5 — Test isolation: ensure daemon processes don't leak between tests
Lot 8 — D3.js CDN usage and graph data embedding in HTML template
Lot 8 — XML/Cypher escaping: proper entity encoding (", <, >) and string escaping
Lot 8 — SVG circular layout: central nodes positioning (inner 150px radius), outer nodes (400px)
Lot 8 — File path handling: sanitizeFilename() for special char replacement (:/?*"<>|)
Lot 8 — Graph.json presence check and graceful error handling when missing
integration_points: Lot 5 — Calls updateVault() and graphVault() callbacks when changes detected
Lot 5 — Uses existing vault/.peter/ directory for state and config storage
Lot 5 — Compatible with vault update/graph commands for incremental rebuilds
Lot 8 — Reads graph.json (703 nodes, 1440 edges in real env)
Lot 8 — All formats use node:fs native modules only (no npm deps)

export_specifications.html: D3.js force-directed graph (237 KB generated), 1200x900px SVG with zoom/pan/hover/click
export_specifications.obsidian: ~500 .md files max with bidirectional [[id]] links, supports vault types
export_specifications.wiki: Hierarchical index.md + type subdirectories, suitable for wiki software
export_specifications.svg: Static circular layout (273 KB), central nodes inner circle (150px), others (400px radius)
export_specifications.graphml: XML standard (451 KB), Gephi/Cytoscape compatible, full entity escaping
export_specifications.neo4j: Cypher scripts (sharded 10k per file), import.sh automation included

next_steps_if_approved: Bump version in package.json and bin/cli.js (currently v0.23.10)
Verify npm publish with new version (tests will run on CI first)
Update website/docs/ with new `vault export` usage examples
Consider Lot 9 (interactive graph UI) or next Phase D task
